mechanism_narrative: A rapid, sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a severe economic contraction is underway, leading to sharp downward revisions of corporate earnings expectations and causing negative equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met during an NBER-dated recession.

mechanism_narrative: A negative trend in retail sales growth indicates weakening consumer demand, which translates directly into lower corporate revenues and earnings for consumer-facing companies, leading to negative broad market returns.
failure_condition: The 12-month rolling correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is rapidly deteriorating (rising UNRATE), cuts to the federal funds rate are perceived as a panicked policy response to a severe crisis rather than effective stimulus, leading to negative equity returns as fear dominates.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both FEDFUNDS is cut and UNRATE is rising sharply over a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a recessionary period signals a collapse in business investment and output, confirming deep economic distress and leading to poor equity performance.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (correctly predicting negative 60-day returns) is below 55% during NBER-dated recessions.

mechanism_narrative: The ongoing collapse in housing starts is a leading indicator of broad economic weakness, financial sector distress, and declining household wealth, negatively impacting the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below 1,000 thousands of units
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05) when HOUST is below 1,000.

mechanism_narrative: A large negative monthly shock in nonfarm payrolls is a high-frequency signal of a rapidly worsening labor market, causing an immediate downward revision of economic growth forecasts and triggering a short-term equity sell-off.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a payrolls drop of >150k is not negative with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is decelerating (falling CPI) while unemployment is rising, it signals a severe demand collapse is underway. This deflationary pressure is negative for equities as it points to a deep recession, overwhelming any positive effect from expected monetary easing.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized rate of change < 1% AND UNRATE 3-month change > 0.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:Commodity prices peaked in mid-2008 and were falling
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day S&P 500 forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: An official report of negative real GDP growth confirms a recession is underway, solidifying negative investor sentiment and leading to further market declines as earnings estimates are broadly cut.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return in the month following the release of a negative GDP report is non-negative.
